preview_text:
The model favors Brazil, assigning a 63% chance of victory compared to Scotland's 12% win probability. Brazil enters as a substantial favorite on the neutral ground, though the underdog retains a meaningful albeit narrow path to an upset.

The draw emerges as the second-most likely outcome in the model's forecast at 25%, suggesting competitive balance in the early-game script despite Brazil's overall edge. The 52-percentage-point gap between the favorites' win probability and the underdog's reflects asymmetry, yet the inclusion of a quarter-chance for a stalemate indicates the match carries genuine competitive uncertainty rather than a predetermined result.

The model and sportsbooks show broad alignment. The market places Brazil at 69% and Scotland at 13%, within 5 percentage points of the model's central estimates. Polymarket prices track similarly. All three sources converge on Brazil as the clear favorite and a plausible draw outcome, though the market marginally downweights the stalemate relative to the model—a common difference across forecast sources.
